name: health_log
invariants: output.ok === false || output.entries.every(e => typeof e.id === 'string' && e.id.length > 0)
output.ok === false || output.entries.every(e => e.detail === undefined || (typeof e.detail === 'object' && e.detail !== null))
output.ok === false || (() => { const ts = output.entries.map(e => e.checked_at); for (let i = 1; i < ts.length; i++) { if (ts[i] > ts[i-1]) return false; } return true; })()
_note: repair.health_log reads the Homeostasis ledger (migration 304 health_check_log) for the operator repair-telemetry dashboard. Against a live migrated DB a valid read returns ok:true with an entries[] array (empty when the monitor has logged nothing), NEVER no_db (no_db is only the headless ctx-without-db path). limit fails CLOSED via badNumericField — a poisoned numeric (NaN/Infinity/negative/>1e6) → ok:false reason invalid_limit, never a silent coercion. A valid limit is then clamped to a SQL LIMIT of min(Number(limit)||50, 200). Entries are newest-first (checked_at DESC); detail_json is parsed onto .detail. disposition optionally filters to one of healed|escalated|noted.
